message: (linear_issues …) takes at most one argument map — e.g. (linear_issues {:id value})

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def pr-ids (distinct (map :closes_linear merged-prs)))\n(count pr-ids)\n(apply linear_issues (vec (for [id pr-ids] {:id id})))"}

message: unknown symbol 'for' — did you mean 'mod'?. Run (tables) to list your capabilities and (describe :name) for one of them.

text: The issue is that `linear_issues` takes a single map, not a vector of maps. Let me check the schema.

data: {"value":{"name":"linear_issues","description":"Linear issues. INSERT creates one; UPDATE (WHERE id=…) changes state/assignee/priority.","volatility":"stable","ops":["read","insert!","update!"],"columns":[{"name":"id","type":"text","description":"e.g. ENG-123"},{"name":"title","type":"text"},{"name"…(+513)

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def pr-ids (distinct (map :closes_linear merged-prs)))
(def issues (apply linear_issues {:id pr-ids}))
(def not-done (filter #(not= (:state %) "done") issues))
not-done"}

message: (linear_issues …) takes at most one argument map — e.g. (linear_issues {:id value})

tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def matches (filter #(and (:closes_linear %) (not-done-ids (:closes_linear %)) (= (:state %) \"merged\")) (github_pull_requests)))\n(count matches)\n(map (fn [p] [(select-keys p [:number :repo :title :closes_linear]) (first (filter #(= (:id %) (:closes_linear p)) all-issues))]) matches)"}

text: The previous `not-done-ids` def stored a vector, not a set. The issue is the old binding persisted. Let me clear and re-do.

message: unknown symbol 'type' — did you mean 'take'?. Run (tables) to list your capabilities and (describe :name) for one of them.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def issue-state (into {} (map (juxt :id :state) all-issues)))\n(map (fn [p] {:pr (:number p) :repo (:repo p) :title (:title p) :closes (:closes_linear p) :linear_state (issue-state (:closes_linear p))}) (sort-by :number matches))"}

text: So maps in this REPL aren't callable. Use `get` instead.
Only the last value is returned. Let me restructure.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def results (map (fn [p] {:pr (:number p) :repo (:repo p) :closes (:closes_linear p) :linear_state (get m1 (:closes_linear p))}) (sort-by :number matches)))\nresults"}
